task_completion.description: Open the Avare app and access the flight plan interface.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the Avare application open with the 'Plan' tab or flight plan screen active.

task_completion.description: Add KPAO as the starting waypoint.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The flight plan list shows 'KPAO' (Palo Alto) as the first entry.

task_completion.description: Add KLVK and KWVI as intermediate waypoints.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The flight plan list shows 'KLVK' (Livermore) followed by 'KWVI' (Watsonville) after the starting waypoint.

task_completion.description: Add KPAO as the final waypoint to complete the round trip.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The flight plan list shows 'KPAO' again at the end of the route, completing the round trip.

task_completion.description: Save the flight plan with the name 'BayAreaTraining'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A save dialog or saved plans list shows the flight plan being saved or already saved with the exact name 'BayAreaTraining'.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the Avare graphical user interface.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the Avare app interface being used, rather than terminal commands or scripts modifying app databases directly.

integrity.description: The flight plan was created in the actual Avare application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The waypoints and plan name are visible within the Avare app's specific layout and menus, not in a generic text editor or note-taking app.

integrity.description: The plan was saved using Avare's internal save feature.
integrity.visual_evidence: The save action occurs via Avare's 'Save' button or menu, rather than renaming a file in a file manager.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The flight plan must contain exactly these four waypoints in order: KPAO, KLVK, KWVI, KPAO. The saved plan name must be exactly 'BayAreaTraining' (case-sensitive, no spaces).
